vitals.location: Viñales, Pinar del Río Province, Cuba
vitals.date: Most recently held: 2025, November 13 (no 2026 date confirmed)
vitals.date_specific: Most recent confirmed edition: November 13, 2025, Viñales
vitals.field_size: ~120 riders expected at the 2024 Caimito launch edition; 2025 Viñales attendance not independently reported

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 64. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 59.

course_description.character: An official L'Eroica-branded vintage cycling event, co-produced with Cuban/Italian travel operators. The inaugural 2024 edition ran from Caimito, Artemisa Province, with a long route of roughly 140km; the event moved to Viñales, Pinar del Río Province for its 2025 edition, with a published route of 115km/740m through northern Pinar del Río and Minas de Matahambre toward Cayo Jutías. Per the official Cuba page's equipment rules, riders may use either an authentic 'Bicicleta Eroica' (built to L'Eroica's Article 6 spec — generally pre-1987 steel, down-tube shifters, toe-clip pedals, external brake cables) or a 'Vintage Style' bicycle — a more flexible category than the strict pre-1987-only rule some sibling Eroica events apply. Vintage or historically-inspired clothing is expected. No 2026 edition currently appears on L'Eroica's official events calendar.
course_description.signature_challenge: Riding period-correct or vintage-style equipment (toe-clip pedals, down-tube shifters, no clipless) through the Viñales Valley's karst limestone landscape — a UNESCO World Heritage cultural landscape, though the organizer hasn't published a detailed surface breakdown (paved vs. unpaved) for this route the way some sibling Eroica events do.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Local options in Viñales Valley and Cayo Jutías area for the current (2025) edition; the 2024 inaugural edition was based in Caimito, Artemisa Province, a different part of the country.

history.origin_story: Inaugural edition held February 10, 2024, starting from Campamento Internacional Julio Antonio Mella in Caimito, Artemisa Province, with Cuban state tour operator Havanatur S.A. as local co-organizer alongside the Italian L'Eroica organization (founder Giancarlo Brocci attended). The event relocated for its second edition, held November 13, 2025, in Viñales, Pinar del Río Province, co-organized with Daiquiri Viaggi and Oscar Rojas, per a public post-event account from an L'Eroica representative. No 2026 edition is confirmed as of this review — the event has run twice in under two years without yet establishing an annual cadence, and Cuba does not appear on L'Eroica's current official events calendar.

biased_opinion.verdict: Real, But Irregular — Two Editions In, No Confirmed Third
biased_opinion.summary: Eroica Cuba is an official L'Eroica-branded event that appears on eroica.cc with MyEroica-style registration, rather than an announced-but-never-launched concept. It has run twice: an inaugural February 2024 edition from Caimito, Artemisa Province (co-organized with Cuban state operator Havanatur), and a second edition in November 2025 that relocated to Viñales, Pinar del Río Province (co-organized with Daiquiri Viaggi and Oscar Rojas). The prior profile conflated details from both editions — Caimito's location with the 2024 edition's ~140km distance, paired with a 2026 date that isn't confirmed anywhere. As of this review, no 2026 edition appears on L'Eroica's official events calendar, and the event has not yet established an annual cadence: two editions in under two years, with the organizing partner changing between them.
strengths: Appears on the official L'Eroica site with MyEroica-style registration, and founder Giancarlo Brocci attended the 2024 launch — this is not an unaffiliated third party simply borrowing the Eroica name
The 2025 edition runs through the Viñales Valley, a UNESCO World Heritage karst landscape — a location distinct from other Eroica events in this database
More flexible equipment rules than some sibling Eroica events — both authentic pre-1987 'Bicicleta Eroica' and broader 'Vintage Style' bicycles are accepted per the official Cuba regulations
Both editions had an established travel-industry co-organizer on the ground (Havanatur in 2024, Daiquiri Viaggi in 2025) rather than running with no local logistical partner at all
weaknesses: No established annual cadence — only two editions in under two years (Feb 2024, Nov 2025), and no 2026 date is confirmed anywhere including L'Eroica's own current events calendar

The local co-organizing partner changed between the only two editions run so far (Havanatur to Daiquiri Viaggi), alongside a full relocation (Caimito to Viñales) — riders researching this event should expect the location, route, and local partner to potentially change again rather than treat any single year's details as fixed

The organizer's own dedicated Cuba page carries a stale planned date from an earlier announcement, and third-party event coverage contains internal date inconsistencies (13 vs. 14 November for the 2025 edition) — a real data-quality problem even at the source
No published surface breakdown (paved vs. unpaved) or gradient data for the route, unlike some sibling Eroica events (e.g. Eroica Dolomiti) that publish detailed course composition
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A real but young Eroica satellite event that hasn't yet settled into a stable annual pattern — the Cuba setting is distinctive, but confirm any current-year date, location, and organizing partner directly with L'Eroica before planning travel.

final_verdict.one_liner: An official L'Eroica vintage-cycling event in Cuba — real, but only two editions in on an irregular schedule, with no 2026 date confirmed.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Only if you can verify a live, current date and location directly with L'Eroica before booking travel — this has already relocated once (Caimito to Viñales) after a single edition and isn't yet a reliable annual fixture.
final_verdict.alternatives: Eroica Dolomiti or Eroica Hispania for a more established, annually reliable Eroica sibling event with published course/surface detail. The original L'Eroica (Tuscany) for the flagship vintage-cycling experience.

catalog_flags.status_note: The prior profile conflated details from two different editions: Caimito, Artemisa Province and a ~140km distance belong to the inaugural Feb 2024 edition; 115km/740m belongs to the Nov 2025 Viñales, Pinar del Río edition. The '2026: February 14' date was unsupported by any source found — no 2026 edition currently appears on L'Eroica's official events calendar. This profile now reflects the most recent CONFIRMED edition (Nov 2025, Viñales) rather than a blended or invented one. Recommend a human call on whether an irregularly-run event (2 editions in under 2 years, no confirmed next date) should carry an 'active' eligibility status or be treated more cautiously in site display, similar to how flandrien-ride and birkebeinerrittet-road flag format/status caveats.

eligibility.notes: IDENTITY/DATE CORRECTION (2026-07-24): the prior profile blended two real editions into one incorrect record. Confirmed history: inaugural edition held Feb 10, 2024 in Caimito, Artemisa Province (~140km long route, co-organized with Havanatur S.A.); second edition held Nov 13, 2025 in Viñales, Pinar del Río Province (115km/740m, co-organized with Daiquiri Viaggi/Oscar Rojas per a public L'Eroica representative's post-event account). This is an official L'Eroica-branded event (appears on eroica.cc with MyEroica-style registration; founder Giancarlo Brocci involved), rather than an unaffiliated licensee. No 2026 edition is confirmed: the organizer's own dedicated Cuba page (eroica.cc/es/cuba) still shows a stale, superseded planned date, and Cuba does not appear on L'Eroica's current official events calendar as of this review. Status set to 'unknown' rather than 'active' or 'defunct' because the event has run twice (2024, 2025) without an established annual cadence and with no evidence of formal discontinuation, but also no confirmed forthcoming edition — a genuinely ambiguous case per the four-state eligibility enum. SOL REVIEW: removed a mislabeled citation (a July 2024 Prensa Latina piece about an unrelated 'Che Eroica' charity fundraiser, not a retrospective on the Feb 2024 edition); softened 'genuine backing'/'not a licensee at arm's length'/'real institutional continuity' hype and unverifiable legal-relationship framing to what's actually sourced (site presence, registration flow, founder attendance); corrected 'irregular, non-annual cadence' framing (only 2 editions exist, so 'no established annual cadence yet' is more accurate than asserting irregularity as a pattern) and reframed the co-organizer change (Havanatur to Daiquiri Viaggi) as a genuine weakness rather than 'institutional continuity,' since the partner actually changed between editions; removed an unverified 'international-events manager' title attribution.
